trade.summary: Brazil exports crude oil, soybeans, gold, and sugar to India. India exports pharmaceuticals, chemicals, and machinery to Brazil. India is one of Brazil's largest trading partners in Asia. Trade has grown substantially in the 2020s.

diplomatic.summary: Brazil and India are fellow BRICS members and coordinate closely on multilateral reform, G20 priorities, and South-South cooperation. Both advocate for a more representative global governance system. Lula and Modi have met multiple times in multilateral settings.

military.summary: No military alliance. Growing defense industry contacts. India's Mahindra and other companies have explored Brazilian defense market. Some information sharing on maritime security.

financial.summary: Growing investment relationship. Indian IT companies (TCS, Wipro, Infosys) have significant operations in Brazil. Brazilian agricultural companies have invested in Indian markets.

energy.summary: India imports Brazilian crude oil and ethanol. Cooperation on biofuels under the Global Biofuels Alliance (both are founding members). Growing energy trade.

scientific.summary: BRICS Science and Technology cooperation framework supports Brazil-India collaboration. Joint research in agriculture, space (ISRO-AEB), and tropical diseases. Growing academic exchanges.
